title: Add Script
description:
do you have any concerns for the below? If not I'll add the other devs to the task.

onestoputah.com

From client:

Currently, we use ServiceTitan as our CRM and would like to have these requests sent directly to our bookings tab in ServiceTitan. There is a way we can accomplish this using a webform scraper built directly into ServiceTitan, which can be implemented on our site, that will automatically have these form submissions appear within our ServiceTitan account. With that said, prior to making this change, I have included a few notes to be aware of to ensure we can confidently make this change to our site.
  • We will want to ensure we are using a supported form provider for the Web Form Attribution to properly connect to our web form. If the form provider is outside of the ones listed, please let us know prior to proceeding. The providers supported are as follows.
    • Gravity Forms
    • Contact Form 7
    • Wordpress Forms
    • Elementor Forms
    • Formidable
    • Wix
    • Scorpion
    • Duda
    • Wufoo
    • Ninja Forms
  • We will want to make sure we do not have onclick handlers on the webform submission buttons. ServiceTitan relies on the form’s default submit event, so we will want to make sure the submit action isn’t blocked or overridden.
  • Lastly, we will want to make sure the form isn't embedded in an iframe. If this is the case, please do not proceed with adding the script.
After reviewing the notes above, if all is well, please use the code provided below and paste it into the page of our website right before the </head> tag. Once this has been completed, please let me know, and I will test and monitor the form submissions from there!

<!-- ServiceTitan DNI -->
<script>
    dni = (function(q,w,e,r,t,y,u){q['ServiceTitanDniObject']=t;q[t]=q[t]||function(){
        (q[t].q=q[t].q||[]).push(arguments)};q[t].l=1*new Date();y=w.createElement(e);
        u=w.getElementsByTagName(e)[0];y.async=true;y.src=r;u.parentNode.insertBefore(y,u);
        return q[t];
    })(window,document,'script','https://static.servicetitan.com/marketing-ads/dni.js','dni');
    dni('init', '1844550432');
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() { dni('load'); }, false);
</script>
<!-- ServiceTitan DNI -->

title: Client is asking for a different booking widget to be integrated
description:
Jeff arranged a call on Friday with me and one of his partners from that private equity group.

The partner had two objectives. He wanted to ask me questions to see if he could determine whether I knew what I was talking about and whether we had "our head up our ass". Apparently, my head was in the correct place on Friday.

He also asked if we could integrate a different booking widget, as the one they use now "sucks".

They are also looking to move away from Scheduler Pro (the native Service Titan scheduler), and ideally, they said they like the one being used on https://www.semperfiheatingcooling.com/

Unfortunately, that is a wordpress site, and it seems what they are using is an integration with Gravity Forms. Given that their site is built on Wordflow, I told them I'd need help looking into what options exist.

Jeff texted me yesterday asking if we had a solution yet. I said I hadn't had enough time.

I need to know what options exist, and also, they are mildly curious what it would cost to convert the site back to wordpress. If there's a way to embed a different scheduling tool I need that sooner, as they don't want to wait for the new site before making the switch.

title: Issues & Explanations
description:
I updated https://onestoputah.com/heating/emergency-furnace-repair 

1. I found how to edit "The #1 Emergency Furnace Repair in Utah with Over 1000+ 5-Star Reviews", but not how to remove it.
2. There's a link on this CTA: "Need emergency furnace repair you can count on? Contact One Stop now! We're here 24/7, whenever you need us.", but since it's the same colour as the text, users won't see it. Is there an easy way to change the link's colour? If not, I can just move "Contact One Stop now!" on another row so it won't be a heading.
3. I updated the first FAQs, but I still see the old ones. Same with "We Offer More Than Just Furnace Repair In Sandy, UT" (it should be "We Offer More Than Just Emergency Furnace Repair" now).
4. I don't know how to change "Our Services" into "Complete HVAC Services" (above We offer more than just...)
5. There's an empty space between "Need emergency furnace repair.." and "What our clients say..". Other pages have the financing banner here, can we also add it here?

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline with previous task - Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites - J389 Go Arco

Here is a sheet we can also use for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.


you can use this as robots.txt:
 
User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google 
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.goarco.com/sitemap_index.xml

Thanks much!

• Post one GBP post bi-weekly or so.
• Update the task's due date for the following week, after posting on GBP.

Post Types
- Client social media posts
- Website blog posts
- 5-star review posts
- Etc.

Also, perform a quick audit of the GBP posts of the competitors every few months or so.

title: Technical Audit Checklist
description:
Run website through Screaming Frog and look for optimization opportunities. Also run the site through GSC to evaluate.

Audit Existing Content
Content Gap Analysis
Link Gap Analysis
URL Structure Audit

Key focus on:
  • Meta Title
  • Meta Description
  • Heading tag structure
  • Orphan Pages

title: Call Tracking Follow Up: WhatConverts
description:
Corey got on a zoom with Chaisey and Jeff and they shared screens. The WhatConverts account isn’t Jeff’s it’s a child account inside CI Web’s account. So he told him he needs to sign up. But when he tried signing up, it says that his ‘email is in use’.

What has to happen is Whatconvert’s support needs to help on this one OR he needs to sign up under a different email.

title: Handover Meeting with SEO Team
description: Confirm w/ SEO Team that they have access and are ready to move to Kick Off Call.

title: Add current monthly budgets (dollar & words amounts) to Project Register...
description:
These will be found in the signed proposal (contract) you uploaded earlier to the CONTRACT DOCS folder in the Client Folder. If they're not there then this is not currently being used:

  1. Column X - Contract Amount (WEBDEV) - Get this from the signed proposal uploaded to the CONTRACT DOCS in Client folder. This is used in Website Development projects only. 
  2. Column Y - LSA Management Fee  - not currently used
  3. Column Z - SmartAds Management Fee - not currently used
  4. Column AA - SmartAds Budget - not currently used
  5. Column AB - Current Monthly Contract Amount ($) - Get this from the signed proposal uploaded to the CONTRACT DOCS in Client folder. This is used for Unfair Advantage SEO projects only.
  6. Column  AC - Current Supplementary Budget ($) - not currently used
  7. Column AD - Current monthly link budget ($) - not currently used
  8. Column AE - Current monthly content budget ($) - not currently used
  9. Column AF - Current monthly content budget (words) - Get this from the signed proposal uploaded to the CONTRACT DOCS in Client folder. Used in Unfair Advantage SEO projects only.

description:

title: Create Basecamp project based on latest appropriate template...
description: Remember to add SoW to the top of the Basecamp project. This is to be manually checked after onboarding call but generally should include SoW, Locations, Service/Case-types & notes from submitted Enquiry Form.

title: Raise project number in Project Register...
description:
1. Change column A from Inactive to Active

  2. Change column E from Active to Inactive

  3. Add entry to column B:
    3.1 Copy Enquiry Number from column E and 'paste special'>'values only' into column B
    3.2 Remove "E" prefix and replace with:
         J, if operation (has no agreed end date, SEO, LSA etc.)
         P, if project (has agreed end date, website development etc.)
         3.21 Change box background color:
                      Green: SEO
                      Yellow: LSA
                      Blue: WebDev

  4. Add project start date in column C - Should usually be today's date
         An easy way to do this would be to copy the date from the cell above, double click and choose today's date from the calendar date picker

  5. Select the cell above in column D and pull the handle down.
         This will automatically add the correct project title column D. This is what you use for the project number in the project doc titles

title: Technical Onboarding
description:
This is a WebFlow based website.  Onboarding items in red will need to be modified for WebFlow.


Web Hosting and Digital Asset Ownership Questions


  1. Domain Ownership
    • Do you own your domain name? If not, who does?
  2. DNS Control
    • Do you have control over your DNS settings (the records that direct your domain to the correct web host)?
  3. Website Platform
    • Is your website built using WordPress?
  4. Content Ownership
    • Do you own all the written content on your website?
  5. Media Licensing
    • Do you own or have the proper licenses for all the images and videos used on your website?
  6. Web Hosting
    • Do you own the web hosting account where your website is currently hosted?
    • Will you need your website to be moved from its current hosting provider?

Tasks


  1. Rebuild or Migrate website as needed
  2. Configure CD/CI & Staging
    1. Staging and GitHub with CD/CI - DEV TEAM
  3. Pull Client Submitted Onboarding Form & Run Through ChatGPT
    1. prompt: Organize this for a basecamp task.  Place all of the website development items together.  Place the IT or tech related items together.  Place all other items together and place this section at the top. <paste onboarding form results> 
  4. Add to Uptime Monitoring
  5. Add Clixsy Plugins
    1. Stream
    2. Simple History
    3. Wordfence
    4. InfiniteWP / MainWP
  6. Add to InfiniteWP / MainWP Dashboard
  7. Httrack Crawl
  8. Wget Crawl
  9. Screaming Frog Crawl
  10. Snapshot of site backup
  11. BuiltWith Report
  12. Form Audit
  13. Phone Number Audit
  14. Run Checklist


Client Submitted Onboarding

(mark items red if they are of note)


DEV Checklist/Audit


  1. Registrar

  2. DNS

  3. Hosting

  4. Client IT team

  5. Add-On Domains

  6. Call Tracking

  7. PHP

  8. GitHub

  9. Staging

  10. CD/CI

  11. ADA Compliance

  12. SMTP

  13. Forms
    1. Software
    2. Stored in Database?
    3. Anti-Spam Strategy
    4. Email Recipient
    5. Emails sent to form submitter?
    6. 3rd Party Integrations
    7. BCC: keith@clixsy.com
  14. Javascript Embeds

  15. Video Hosting Provider
  16. Theme
    1. Name
    2. Child
    3. Up to date?
    4. Customizations?
  17. Chatbot

  18. Plugins of note

  19. Uptime Robot

  20. Licenses

  21. Backup Plan

  22. Page builder

  23. Custom Post Type
    1. List
    2. Check SEO settings & sitemaps
  24. Review Embeds

  25. Current Cache/Optimization Strategy

  26. Tools > Site Health

  27. PSI
    1. desktop - 
    2. mobile - 
  28. Core Vitals

  29. Prev Agency emails

  30. Prev Agency artifacts

  31. Site Admin

  32. Notes

Pass Audit Results to Client / Account Manager

title: Branded Keyword Identification
description:

Record and strategize on how to increase CTR

title: Setup BL & GeoGrids
description: Add client to Bright Local and set up the rank tracking and Geo Grid reports.

title: YouTube Channel Optimization
description:
Check with leads if we are to optimize client's YouTube channel before starting task.

This task requires access to client's YouTube channel, they need to add tempclixsyreports@gmail.com as a manager of YT channel.

This task will be for optimizing the overall channel itself, which includes but not limited to:

- Channel
     - about
     - handle
     - channel art, branding, and links
     - channel page layout
- Playlists
- First 10 videos (if applicable, as some new clients we offer the first 10 videos to be included in the YT channel optimization)
